summary: This bundle records deterministic proof that agent status exposes safe top-level orientation branches, including declared runner smoke, while keeping ready-runner eval execution out of first-touch top-level branches.

evidenceScope.targetSurface: agent status top-level nextBranches and runnerReadiness.nextBranch boundary
evidenceScope.claimBoundary: Only the safe branch catalog boundary in docs/contracts/runner-readiness.md:118 is marked satisfied. This does not prove that agent status chooses an eval fixture for a selected claim.
notClaimed: first-touch agent status may launch eval, review, optimize, edits, or commits
every future branch id is covered automatically without tests

protectedBehaviors: typed runner smoke_command_template is preserved as smokeCommandTemplate
BuildRunnerReadiness exposes a non-executing run_runner_smoke branch with the declared smoke command
agent status top-level nextBranches include run_runner_smoke when the selected typed runner declares smoke_command_template
agent status top-level nextBranches do not promote run_eval_with_assessed_runner
runner-readiness branches keep stable branch shape fields
first-touch agent status exposes claim discovery orientation
first-touch agent status does not offer eval test, review variants, optimize, or git commit
runner-readiness contract remains specdown-valid after the safe branch wording change

decision.reason: Focused runtime and app tests prove that safe top-level orientation branches include the declared runner smoke command while ready-runner eval remains nested under runnerReadiness instead of being promoted into first-touch nextBranches.
